root_cause_identification.expected_solution: Agent must identify that Flux v2.2 / helm-controller v0.37 changed the HelmRelease status condition API. helm-controller's reconciliation pipeline (internal/controller/helmrelease_controller.go and internal/reconcile/release.go, with HelmRelease type definitions in api/v2beta2/helmrelease_types.go) writes status conditions in the new format after a successful release. HelmRelease resources that were in-progress during the v0.36 -> v0.37 upgrade retain old-format conditions, and the new reconciler in internal/controller/helmrelease_controller.go cannot interpret them, so it perpetually treats the release as not ready and re-reconciles in a loop.
evaluation_criteria: Names internal/controller/helmrelease_controller.go (helm-controller) as the reconciler that misparses old-format conditions
Names internal/reconcile/release.go (helm-controller) as the writer of new-format conditions
References api/v2beta2/helmrelease_types.go (helm-controller) as the type/API surface that changed
Explains the version-mismatch mechanism: stale old-format conditions left on in-progress HelmRelease resources after the upgrade
Identifies why this produces an infinite reconcile loop rather than a transient error

error_chain_trace.expected_solution: Error chain: (1) A HelmRelease was in-progress when helm-controller was upgraded from v0.36 to v0.37 (Flux v2.1 -> v2.2). (2) helm-controller's internal/reconcile/release.go successfully deploys the release and writes new-format status conditions defined in api/v2beta2/helmrelease_types.go. (3) Old-format conditions from before the upgrade remain on the HelmRelease resource. (4) The reconciler internal/controller/helmrelease_controller.go reads the status conditions and cannot parse the old format, so it treats the release as not ready. (5) The reconciler triggers another reconciliation, producing an infinite loop. (6) kustomize-controller and Flux CLI consumers, which depend on HelmRelease readiness, are also blocked.
evaluation_criteria: Mentions the helm-controller v0.36 -> v0.37 (Flux v2.1 -> v2.2) upgrade as the trigger for in-progress HelmReleases retaining old-format conditions
Identifies helm-controller writing new-format conditions and the same controller's reconciler reading them as the seam
Describes the infinite reconcile loop and the downstream impact on kustomize-controller / Flux CLI

affected_resources.expected_solution: Affected components: helm-controller reconciler that parses status conditions (internal/controller/helmrelease_controller.go); helm-controller release writer that emits new-format conditions (internal/reconcile/release.go); the HelmRelease v2beta2 API surface (api/v2beta2/helmrelease_types.go and api/v2beta2/condition_types.go); all HelmRelease resources that were in-progress during the v0.36 to v0.37 helm-controller upgrade; kustomize-controller and Flux CLI consumers that gate on HelmRelease readiness.
evaluation_criteria: Mentions HelmRelease resources that were in-progress during the upgrade as affected
Mentions a downstream consumer such as kustomize-controller

remediation_proposal.expected_solution: Add backward-compatible status condition parsing in helm-controller's reconciler (internal/controller/helmrelease_controller.go) so it accepts both old and new condition formats. For immediate operational relief, manually delete and recreate the affected HelmRelease resources or annotate them to force a full status condition reset.
evaluation_criteria: Identifies an operational workaround (recreate or annotate affected HelmRelease resources)
Addresses the parser/writer skew rather than only suppressing the symptom
